title: Stonehenge's 6-Ton Altar Stone Was Dragged 700 Kilometers From Scotland
script: How did this get HERE? Scientists announced this week that Stonehenge's Altar Stone — a 6-ton sandstone slab at the center of the monument — was not quarried locally. A new study published in the Journal of Quaternary Science traced the stone's mineral fingerprint to the Caithness coast in northern Scotland, 700 kilometers away. That means ancient Britons moved a 13,000-pound rock the equivalent distance from London to Amsterdam, roughly 5,000 years ago. Glaciers were ruled out because no ice-sheet model could have carried the stone that far south. Researchers at Curtin University say the transport was deliberately planned. Stonehenge's famous bluestones came from Wales — but the Altar Stone's journey was far longer. Why would people drag a 6-ton stone across all of ancient Britain just to put it at the center of Stonehenge?
